attributes.title: Address
attributes.readMe:
# Address Field Definition The `Address` field definition provides a reusable way to represent physical addresses within a Boxel application. It allows you to capture various address components such as street, city, state, postal code, and country, and automatically compute a formatted full address string. ## Import To use the `Address` field in your code, import it from the `../address` module: ```javascript import { Address } from '../address'; ``` ## Define Field You can define an `Address` field within a card definition using the `contains` method: ```javascript export class MyCard extends CardDef { @field address = contains(Address); } ``` ## Invoke Template In your card templates, you can display the address field using the `@fields.address` syntax: ```hbs
<@fields.address />
``` ## Dependencies The `Address` field definition has the following dependencies: - Imports `CountryField` from `./country` - Uses `EntityDisplayWithIcon` and `MapPinIcon` components from `./components/entity-icon-display` ## Usage and Examples Here's an example of how you might use the `Address` field in your application: ```javascript export class UserProfile extends CardDef { @field address = contains(Address); } ``` In the template for the `UserProfile` card: ```hbs

User Details

Address

<@fields.address />
``` The `Address` field will automatically compute a formatted full address string based on the individual address components, and display it in the template.
